articles.description: A human really borks things at Anthropic for the second time this week.

articles.title: Salesforce announces an AI-heavy makeover for Slack, with 30 new features
articles.description: Slack just got a whole lot more useful.

articles.title: Robotaxi companies refuse to say how often their AVs need remote help
articles.description: Aurora, May Mobility, Motional, Nuro, Tesla, Waymo, and Zoox all refused to cough up a number during Senator Ed Markey's recent investigation.

articles.title: OpenAI, not yet public, raises $3B from retail investors in monster $122B fund raise
articles.description: OpenAI's latest funding round, led by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, values the AI lab at $852 billion as it nears an IPO.

articles.title: It’s not your imagination: AI seed startups are commanding higher valuations
articles.description: Among the most recent Y Combinator cohort, many startups were commanding $40 million valuations. But with more money comes higher expectations.

articles.title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
articles.description: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

articles.title: Meta was finally held accountable for harming teens. Now what?
articles.description: Meta faces thousands more court cases while Congress has proposed numerous bills addressing children’s online safety, some heavily criticized.

articles.title: Go play this secret game in your TikTok DMs
articles.description: The game is available globally and can be accessed in both one-on-one DM chats and in group chats.

articles.title: Caller ID app Truecaller hits 500 million monthly users
articles.description: Truecaller now has more than 4 million paying users across the world.

articles.title: Social gaming platform Rec Room, once valued at $3.5B, is shutting down
articles.description: Rec Room, the social platform for user-generated games and virtual experiences, will shutter on June 1.

articles.title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
articles.description: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

articles.title: Whoop’s valuation just tripled to $10 billion
articles.description: The fitness tracking startup just closed a $575 million Series G with Cristiano Ronaldo and LeBron James among its investors. The obvious question looming over a round of this size at this valuation: Is an IPO coming?

articles.title: Uber and WeRide ramp up robotaxi operations in Dubai
articles.description: Uber and WeRide have launched robotaxi operations without a human safety operator in Dubai as part of a broader expansion in the Middle East.

articles.title: North Korean hackers blamed for hijacking popular Axios open source project to spread malware
articles.description: A hacker inserted malware in Axios, an open source web tool downloaded tens of millions of times weekly, in a widespread hack.

articles.title: FedEx chooses partnerships over proprietary tech for its automation strategy
articles.description: FedEx recently announced a partnership with Berkshire Gray as the company works with external players to develop its automation tech.

articles.title: Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
articles.description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

articles.title: Health data giant CareCloud says hackers accessed patients’ medical records
articles.description: CareCloud, a major provider of medical records storage, said hackers accessed one of its repositories of patient data earlier in March. It provides technology for more than 45,000 providers covering millions of patients.

articles.title: Rivian spinoff Also will build autonomous delivery vehicles for DoorDash
articles.description: DoorDash joins Greenoaks Capital in another $200 million funding round for Also, bringing its total funding to more than $500 million.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Axios compromised on NPM – Malicious versions drop remote access trojan
URL: https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/axios-compromised-on-npm-malicious-versions-drop-remote-access-trojan
Supply chain attack on one of npm's most downloaded packages delivers RAT payload.
Axios sees tens of millions of weekly downloads—this is a tier-one supply chain compromise with blast radius across enterprise JavaScript stacks. Attribution pointing to North Korean threat actors fits the pattern of state-sponsored attacks targeting developer infrastructure. Every fund with software portfolio companies should be auditing dependency chains today.

### Show HN: 1-Bit Bonsai, the First Commercially Viable 1-Bit LLMs
URL: https://prismml.com/
Extreme quantization hits production-ready status, collapsing inference costs.
1-bit models represent the logical endpoint of the efficiency push—if viable, this fundamentally changes the economics of edge AI deployment. Watch for implications on Nvidia's data center margins and the competitive moat of frontier labs. The "commercially viable" framing suggests this is beyond research curiosity.

### Show HN: Postgres extension for BM25 relevance-ranked full-text search
URL: https://github.com/timescale/pg_textsearch
Timescale brings native BM25 search to Postgres, eating into Elasticsearch's lunch.
This continues the "Postgres for everything" trend that's consolidating developer tooling. For PE, this signals compression in the search infrastructure market—why pay for standalone search when your primary database handles it? Timescale positioning here is smart competitive moating.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### OpenAI closes funding round at an $852B valuation
URL: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/31/openai-funding-round-ipo.html
OpenAI now valued higher than Meta was pre-pandemic, IPO imminent.
$852B makes this the most valuable private company in history by a wide margin. The $122B raise with $3B from retail investors signals IPO preparation—they're building a public market constituency. Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank leading suggests strategic positioning trumps pure financial returns for participants.

### Social gaming platform Rec Room, once valued at $3.5B, is shutting down
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/social-gaming-platform-rec-room-once-valued-at-3-5b-is-shutting-down/
Peak-era VR bet vaporizes—$3.5B to zero in the metaverse winter.
June 1 shutdown is a clean kill, not a zombie pivot. This is the clearest signal yet that 2021-vintage metaverse valuations were catastrophically wrong. Secondary market implications for any remaining social VR positions are severe.

### Whoop's valuation just tripled to $10 billion
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/whoop-valuation-10b-series-g-fundraise/
Hardware fitness tracker hits $10B on $575M Series G, celebrity investors signal consumer brand premium.
Ronaldo and LeBron as investors is marketing disguised as cap table management. The tripling suggests either dramatic revenue acceleration or IPO-prep pricing. At $10B, they're pricing in Garmin-level outcomes—worth watching whether the subscription economics actually support that.

### Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto's Chris Dixon
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/yupp-ai-shuts-down-33m-a16z-crypto-chris-dixon/
a16z-backed AI feedback startup burns through $33M in under a year.
Another data point on the "AI startup mortality rate" chart. Crowdsourced model feedback couldn't find product-market fit despite top-tier backing. The speed of failure—sub-12 months—suggests either unit economics never worked or the market moved too fast.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### The Claude Code Source Leak: fake tools, frustration regexes, undercover mode
URL: https://alex000kim.com/posts/2026-03-31-claude-code-source-leak/
Leaked Anthropic code reveals internal tooling architecture and user sentiment detection.
"Frustration regexes" and "undercover mode" point to sophisticated behavioral engineering in AI assistants. This leak, combined with TechCrunch's "Anthropic is having a month" coverage, suggests operational security issues at a frontier lab. Due diligence implications for any AI infrastructure investments.

### Robotaxi companies refuse to say how often their AVs need remote help
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/robotaxi-companies-refuse-to-say-how-often-their-avs-need-remote-help/
Seven major AV players stonewalled Senator Markey on human intervention metrics.
The refusal to disclose is the story—Aurora, Waymo, Tesla, and peers all know these numbers would damage the "autonomous" narrative. For anyone modeling robotaxi unit economics, the human teleoperation cost is the hidden variable that determines whether the math works.

### FedEx chooses partnerships over proprietary tech for its automation strategy
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/fedex-chooses-partnerships-over-proprietary-tech-for-its-automation-strategy/
Logistics giant goes buy-not-build on warehouse automation, partners with Berkshire Gray.
This is a strategic signal for the robotics sector: even $80B logistics operators are deciding the tech isn't core. Good for robotics pure-plays like Berkshire Gray; bad for anyone betting on vertical integration at incumbents. The partnership model implies commoditization of warehouse automation.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The OpenAI valuation and Rec Room shutdown in the same news cycle perfectly frames where we are: AI is absorbing all available capital while adjacent tech bets from the last cycle get written off. The Axios supply chain attack is the kind of infrastructure vulnerability that will eventually force a reckoning on open source dependency management—this is systemic risk hiding in plain sight. Meanwhile, the robotaxi disclosure fight and 1-bit LLM developments both point to the same underlying question: how much of the AI/autonomy narrative is real economics versus capital markets storytelling?
